title: The Magicians of Caprona - Chapter 6 (Fragment 5)
text:
There were murmurs of appreciation from the rest of the family. Antonio took the pear and sniffed it. And he smiled, to Marco’s evident relief. “Good fruit,” he said. “That was very elegantly done, Signor Andretti. But there is one more thing I must ask you. Would you agree to change your name to Montana? That is our custom, you see.”

“Yes, Rosa told me,” said Marco. “And—and this is a difficulty. My brother needs me in his firm, and he too wants to keep his family name. Would it be all right if I’m known as Montana when I’m here, and as—as Andretti when I’m at home with my brother?”

“You mean you and Rosa wouldn’t live here?” Antonio asked, astonished.

“Not all the time. No,” said Marco. From the way he said it, it was clear he was not going to change his mind.

This was serious. Antonio looked at Old Niccolo. And there were grave faces all round at the thought of the family being broken up.

“I don’t see why they shouldn’t,” said Elizabeth.

“Well—my great-uncle did it,” Old Niccolo said. “But it was not a success. His wife ran off to Sicily with a greasy little warlock.”

“That doesn’t mean I’m going to!” Rosa said.

The family wavered, with the tree gently rattling in their midst. Everyone loved Rosa. Marco was clearly nice. Nobody wanted to break their hearts. But this idea of living away from the Casa—!

Aunt Francesca heaved herself forward, saying, “I side with Elizabeth. Our Rosa has found herself a nice boy with more talent and a better voice than I’ve seen outside our family for years. Let them get married.”

Antonio looked dreadfully worried at this, but he did not pull his lip. He seemed to be relaxing, ready to agree, when Rinaldo set the tree rattling furiously by pushing his way underneath it.

“Just a moment. Aren’t we all being a bit trustful? Who is this fellow, after all? Why haven’t we come across him and his talents before?”

Paolo hung his head and watched Rinaldo under his hair. This was Rinaldo in the mood he least admired. Rinaldo loud and aggressive, with an unpleasant twist to his mouth. Rinaldo was still a little pale from the cut on his head, but this went rather well with the black clothes and the red brigand’s scarf. Rinaldo knew it did. He flung up his head with an air, and contemptuously brushed off a petal that had fallen on his black sleeve. And he looked at Marco, challenging him to answer.

The way Marco looked back showed that he was quite ready to stand up to Rinaldo. “I’ve been at college in Rome until recently,” he said. “If that’s what you mean.”

Rinaldo swung round to face the family. “So he says,” he said. “He’s done a pretty trick for us, and said all the right things—but so would anyone in his place.” He swung round on Marco. It was so dramatic that Tonino winced and even Paolo felt a little unhappy. “I don’t trust you,” said Rinaldo. “I’ve seen your face before somewhere.”

“At the Old Bridge,” said Marco.

“No, not there. It was somewhere else,” said Rinaldo.

And this must be true, Tonino realized. Marco did have a familiar look. And Tonino could not have seen him at the Old Bridge, because Tonino had never been there.

“Do you want me to fetch my brother, or my priest, to vouch for me?” asked Marco.

“No,” said Rinaldo rudely. “I want the truth.”

Marco took a deep breath. “I don’t want to be unfriendly,” he said. The arm Rosa was not holding bent, and so did the fist on the end of it. Rinaldo gave it a look as if he welcomed it, and swaggered a step nearer.

vocabulary.definition: ready or likely to attack or confront; forceful
vocabulary.example: Rinaldo loud and aggressive, with an unpleasant twist to his mouth.

vocabulary.definition: recognition and enjoyment of the good qualities of someone or something
vocabulary.example: There were murmurs of appreciation from the rest of the family.

vocabulary.definition: very surprised or amazed
vocabulary.example: Antonio asked, astonished.

vocabulary.definition: inviting or demanding a challenge; testing abilities
vocabulary.example: He looked at Marco, challenging him to answer.

vocabulary.definition: expressing disdain or disrespect
vocabulary.example: He contemptuously brushed off a petal.

vocabulary.definition: a usual way of behaving or a traditional practice
vocabulary.example: That is our custom, you see.

vocabulary.definition: a problem or something that is hard to do or understand
vocabulary.example: And—and this is a difficulty.

vocabulary.definition: sudden and striking; full of emotion or excitement
vocabulary.example: It was so dramatic that Tonino winced.

vocabulary.definition: very badly or severely; extremely
vocabulary.example: Antonio looked dreadfully worried at this.

vocabulary.definition: in a graceful and stylish manner
vocabulary.example: That was very elegantly done, Signor Andretti.

vocabulary.definition: in an extremely angry or intense manner
vocabulary.example: Rinaldo set the tree rattling furiously by pushing his way underneath it.

vocabulary.definition: very serious; needing careful consideration
vocabulary.example: And there were grave faces all round at the thought of the family being broken up.

vocabulary.definition: to lift or throw with effort; or to breathe out heavily
vocabulary.example: Aunt Francesca heaved herself forward.

vocabulary.definition: soft, indistinct sounds made by a group of people talking quietly
vocabulary.example: There were murmurs of appreciation from the rest of the family.

vocabulary.definition: giving a sense of happy satisfaction or enjoyment
vocabulary.example: Marco was clearly nice.

vocabulary.definition: making a rapid succession of short sharp sounds
vocabulary.example: The tree gently rattling in their midst.

vocabulary.definition: to become less tense or anxious
vocabulary.example: He seemed to be relaxing, ready to agree.

vocabulary.definition: to support or agree with one person or group against another
vocabulary.example: I side with Elizabeth.

vocabulary.definition: a confident and arrogant walk or manner
vocabulary.example: Rinaldo knew it did. He flung up his head with an air.

vocabulary.definition: to walk or behave in a very confident, sometimes arrogant way
vocabulary.example: He swaggered a step nearer.

vocabulary.definition: a natural ability to do something well
vocabulary.example: Our Rosa has found herself a nice boy with more talent and a better voice.

vocabulary.definition: willing to trust others; believing that people are honest and good
vocabulary.example: Aren’t we all being a bit trustful?

vocabulary.definition: not friendly; hostile or cold
vocabulary.example: I don’t want to be unfriendly.

vocabulary.definition: to give a guarantee or assurance of something
vocabulary.example: Do you want me to fetch my brother, or my priest, to vouch for me?

vocabulary.definition: to be indecisive or fluctuate between choices or opinions
vocabulary.example: The family wavered, with the tree gently rattling in their midst.
